troubleshooting.title: Database Error on Launch or During Use
troubleshooting.shortdesc: Portfolio Manager fails to start or displays an error message related to the database. Most database errors are caused by a locked file, a missing directory, or a failed schema migration.

condition.p: One or more of the following symptoms occur:

ul.li: A dialog shows "Database error" on launch.
The application starts but projects, sessions, or milestones do not display.
The log file contains sqlite3.OperationalError entries.
The application crashes when creating or saving data.

p.b: The database directory does not exist or is not writable.

step.cmd: Verify that ~/.portfolio_manager/ exists and is writable:
If the directory does not exist, create it:
Relaunch Portfolio Manager. It will recreate the database automatically.

p.b: A failed schema migration corrupted the database.

step.cmd: If a backup file exists, restore it:

step.stepresult: The migration runs again. If it fails a second time, check the log file for the specific error and report it to the project repository.

p.b: The database file is locked by another process.

step.cmd: Check whether another Portfolio Manager instance is already running and close it.
If no other instance is running, verify no orphan process holds the database lock:
Terminate any process listed, then relaunch Portfolio Manager.
